## Overview
Plan, schedule, and prepare for the final exam in CS-5368 Intelligent Systems. This includes reviewing course materials, creating study guides, and organizing exam preparation schedule.

## Course Information
- **Course**: CS-5368 Intelligent Systems
- **Semester**: Spring 2026 (or as applicable)
- **Exam Type**: Final Exam
- **Status**: Planning phase

## Exam Planning Tasks

### Scheduling
- [ ] Confirm final exam date and time from course syllabus/Canvas
- [ ] Check for any exam location or format requirements
- [ ] Block out study time in calendar
- [ ] Plan study schedule leading up to exam
- [ ] Coordinate with other course commitments

### Content Review
- [ ] Review all course materials (lectures, slides, notes)
- [ ] Identify key topics and concepts to focus on
- [ ] Review assignments and projects for important concepts
- [ ] Check for any study guides or review materials provided
- [ ] Review blog posts written for the course (if applicable)

### Study Materials Preparation
- [ ] Create comprehensive study guide
- [ ] Organize notes by topic
- [ ] Create summary sheets for key concepts
- [ ] Review and understand all algorithms covered
- [ ] Practice problems and examples
- [ ] Review any code implementations from assignments

### Exam Format Preparation
- [ ] Understand exam format (multiple choice, short answer, problems, etc.)
- [ ] Review exam policies (open book, notes allowed, time limits, etc.)
- [ ] Practice with similar problem types
- [ ] Time management practice if applicable
- [ ] Prepare any allowed materials (notes, formula sheets, etc.)

## Course Topics to Review

Based on CS-5368 Intelligent Systems course, review the following topics:

### Core Topics
- [ ] Introduction to Intelligent Systems
- [ ] Search algorithms (BFS, DFS, A*, etc.)
- [ ] Game playing and adversarial search
- [ ] Constraint satisfaction problems
- [ ] Knowledge representation
- [ ] Reasoning and inference
- [ ] Planning and decision making
- [ ] Machine learning basics (if covered)
- [ ] Agent architectures
- [ ] Multi-agent systems (if covered)

### Algorithms and Techniques
- [ ] Search algorithm implementations
- [ ] Heuristic functions
- [ ] Constraint propagation
- [ ] Inference algorithms
- [ ] Planning algorithms
- [ ] Any specific algorithms from assignments

### Practical Applications
- [ ] Case studies covered in class
- [ ] Project implementations
- [ ] Real-world applications
- [ ] Problem-solving strategies

## Deliverables

1. **Study Schedule**: Calendar with blocked study times
2. **Study Guide**: Comprehensive review document
3. **Summary Sheets**: Quick reference materials
4. **Practice Problems**: Solved practice problems
5. **Exam Preparation**: Ready for exam day

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Exam date and time confirmed
- [ ] Study schedule created and blocked in calendar
- [ ] All course materials reviewed
- [ ] Study guide created and organized
- [ ] Key concepts understood and can be explained
- [ ] Practice problems completed
- [ ] Any allowed materials prepared (notes, formula sheets)
- [ ] Ready for exam day

## Timeline

- **Planning Start**: [Immediate]
- **Study Period**: [1-2 weeks before exam]
- **Exam Date**: [To be confirmed]
- **Priority**: High (final exam)

## Notes

- Start planning early to allow adequate study time
- Break down large topics into manageable study sessions
- Focus on understanding concepts, not just memorization
- Practice explaining concepts to ensure deep understanding
- Review assignments and projects for practical applications
- Form study groups if helpful (if allowed)
- Get adequate rest before exam day
- Prepare all necessary materials the day before
- Arrive early on exam day
